blog.name: 49-ibr
blog.title: 49: A Multiverse
blog.description:
Hello! 
Welcome to 49: a multiverse of 49 magics, focused mainly on a series of queer fantasy novellas, written by @isabellebissonrouthier! They are aimed at adults, but there is no explicit content in the main series. The books of that main series follow their own main characters, navigating unique magics, gifted by gods, as they traverse their world of Ungode. And there's so much more on the way... like a mythological exploration of this magic system, a contemporary (explicit) romance with some of the characters, a superhero series, a magic academy, a world of horror, and so much more! 
49: VOLUME 01 and 49: VOLUME 02 and 49: THE CAPITAL OF UNGODE by Isabelle Bisson-Routhier are out now on Amazon!
I also have a sideblog (@i-am-49) where I roleplay as my characters!

tags: anyway who wants to ask about different characters and how I think they cry
response.summary: This might just be me but does anyone else have a list of how various characters cry? Because I do, and I don't know how much of...
response.body:
This might just be me but does anyone else have a list of how various characters cry? Because I do, and I don’t know how much of an angst writer thing that is.

It’s a very comprehensive list, covering what emotions at what intensities would make them cry, how they cry, the emotion it presents as to other people. I have a template for sake of ease. I do it for every character I create, every character I play, most characters I write fanfiction for, and a few extras I find interesting. It’s a good character study.

Yes there are differences. If you think that everyone cries the same way, then you are a liar, projecting, or haven’t seen a whole lot of people cry. Or you are rage baiting me on purpose for evil reasons, and if that is the case, I hope you get put in a sandwich and eaten by a messy toddler with gross sticky fingers and very few table manners.

blog.description: "Destruction, like creation, is one of nature's mandates." - Marquis de Sade

response.summary:
“I crave your mouth, your voice, your hair.
Silent and starving, I prowl through the streets.
Bread does not nourish me, dawn...
response.body:
“I crave your mouth, your voice, your hair.
Silent and starving, I prowl through the streets.
Bread does not nourish me, dawn disrupts me, all day
I hunt for the liquid measure of your steps.”
- Pablo Neruda, 100 Love Sonnets

Paolo e Virginia by Alessandro Puttinati

response.body:
Cover art ideas

why write the books when I could procraste and draw sketches for the cover art?

I was really inspired by like fairy tale covers

Book two title reveal?

Theres saposed to be three books but the sketch for three isnt done yet.

This is one of the main reasons I want to self publish. Authors actually dont get alot of say over covers in trad publishing and I think if I got stuck with a bad title I would turn to hysterics….also I want freind to do some illustrations for me :/

response.summary:
49: VOLUME 01 by Isabelle Bisson-Routhier is out on Amazon now!
THREE UNIQUE TALES. ONE FANTASY WORLD. A SECRET TO UNCOVER....
response.body:
49: VOLUME 01 by Isabelle Bisson-Routhier is out on Amazon now!

THREE UNIQUE TALES. ONE FANTASY WORLD. A SECRET TO UNCOVER.

Aveline, the princess, wants freedom from her gilded birdcage.

Chrysanthemum, the hero, trusts too many vile villains and beasts.

Lucah, the pirate, will follow his blood-drinking captain until the end.

How will their love destroy them?

She/Her. 23. Bisexual. 
I'm a writer, and the first four books of my multiverse, 49, are now on Amazon! The sideblog for the series is @49-ibr. 
I post mostly writeblr content! 
My tiktok, youtube, insta, and ao3 have the same username. 
I love messages, asks, and tag games!
My banner & icon are original characters commissioned from @theoasiswinds

response.summary: “She can ask as many times as she wants, and I’ll always say we’re okay as many times as she needs. Even if we’re not. I’ll say...
response.body: “She can ask as many times as she wants, and I’ll always say we’re okay as many times as she needs. Even if we’re not. I’ll say it over and over again until she’s sick of it.” —Aurora Wright from my wlw dystopian book “Not Your Dog” (Also, yes, I know this is problematic because you should be honest with your feelings—it’s meant to be a character flaw of hers LOL.)

response.summary: 3rd Pride month post: the labels that my book characters have!
response.body:
3rd Pride month post: the labels that my book characters have!

(lore note: out of the things that survived from archaic times (our present), these sorts of labels didn’t. These characters wouldn’t use these labels in the canonical books, but that’s because they don’t know about them. All labels ascribed to each character are canonical, they just don’t use them in book because they don’t know what to call how they feel)

Nathair

Gender: trans-man

Sexual orientation: ace

Romantic orientation: achillean

Yosashi

Gender: cis male

Sexual orientation: gay

Romantic orientation: demi

Suzu

Gender: trans man

Sexual orientation: gay

romantic orientation: andro-alterous

Vyro

Gender: cis woman

Sexual orientation: demi

romantic orientation: sapphic

Luno

Gender: genderfae

Sexual orientation: ace

romantic orientation: lesbian

Heron

Gender: cis male

Sexual orientation: bisexual

romantic orientation: demiromantic

Larme

Gender: cis woman

Sexual orientation: hetero

romantic orientation: straight

Azizi

Gender: cis male but fem presenting

Sexual orientation: gay

romantic orientation: demi

Darius

Gender: cis male

Sexual orientation: Azizi-sexual (no, literally, he’s only ever been attracted to his man like that)/

romantic orientation: gay

Mol

Gender: cis male

Sexual orientation: ace

romantic orientation: omniromantic (leaning masc (in presentation specifically, not gender)

Atlas

Gender: cis male

Sexual orientation: pansexual

romantic orientation: panromantic

response.body:
Regan Talley

New Writer Interview on Substack!

Today’s interview is especially meaningful because I’m introducing one of my oldest and dearest friends, Regan Talley.

Born and raised in Los Angeles, Regan is currently working on a powerful new play titled SHEPHARD/TILL. Written in verse, the piece examines the intersection of race and sexuality through the lens of two of the most significant hate crimes in American history. It seeks to explore understanding, prejudice, and the bridges that can be built between communities often divided by fear and misunderstanding.

Read the full interview now ⬇️

Regan Talley

response.body:
One thing I know for sure is that readers and writers look at books very differently. Most readers don’t care who published a book. They don’t care about rankings, algorithms, or where an author falls in some unofficial hierarchy. They pick up a book because the story interests them. They either connect with it or they don’t.

Gatekeeping in Writing Communities

blog.name: attoviaplays
blog.title: Attovia
blog.description:
I'm a game developer who writes, world builds, and DMs in his free time. Let's Build Together!


Check out my wiki
If you want to share and chat with fellow world builders join us in the Attovia discord!

response.body:
Build Together Day 493

Hey World Builders! Its Build Together day 493. Today’s prompt comes from Kendamarania in the discord: How is magic integrated in daily life in your world?

Remember if you want to share your own response or hang out with fellow world builders you are more than welcome to join us in our discord!

Join the Attovia Discord Server!

response.body:
Writing Prompt #418

To secure a political alliance between two warring villages, the village leaders agree on an arranged marriage between two candidates of their choice.

Both were sent as assassins, unaware that their spouse was assigned the same mission.

response.body:
49: VOLUME 02 by Isabelle Bisson-Routhier is out on Amazon now!

Netharia, once a noble hero, now an elderly mother, is haunted by the memories and followers of her villainous ex-wife.

Scylla, a circus ringmaster, commands the minds and the bodies of the dozens of dancers in her troupe.

Magnus, an assassin with a charming smile, will do anything to keep his favourite detective at his heels.

blog.name: isabellebissonrouthier
blog.title: Isabelle Bisson-Routhier
blog.description:
Hi, I'm Isabelle! 
She/Her. 23. Bisexual. 
I'm a writer, and the first four books of my multiverse, 49, are now on Amazon! The sideblog for the series is @49-ibr. 
I post mostly writeblr content! 
My tiktok, youtube, insta, and ao3 have the same username. 
I love messages, asks, and tag games!
My banner & icon are original characters commissioned from @theoasiswinds

blog.name: livewirestories
blog.title: Livewire Stories
blog.description:
Livewire Multimedia is a production company run by Gwen Fulmen creating podcasts, books, games, and short films.
This blog is run by Gwen Fulmen and her husband Theo

response.summary: Somewhere in Amity City, some darkened building, some disgusting corner… a person is about to draw their last breath. Who do you...
response.body:
Somewhere in Amity City, some darkened building, some disgusting corner… a person is about to draw their last breath. Who do you call when no one can save you? Who gets your last words?

Dial Tone Terminus Episode 7 comes out today at 2:00 PM PT on YouTube and Spotify.
